name: A scheduled task was created
applicable_version: Windows Server 2016 and later; Windows 10 and later
definition: Windows Security Event ID 4698 records creation of a scheduled task and supports detection of task-based persistence and execution.
trigger_scenarios: The event is written when a scheduled task is registered and Task Scheduler auditing is enabled.

key_fields.explanation: The task path and name. Hidden-looking names, names that imitate Microsoft tasks, and new tasks on servers are useful pivots.
The XML task definition includes command, arguments, trigger, principal, and run level.
The creating account. Review whether its role and logon context authorize task creation.

false_positives: Enterprise management agents and software updaters create scheduled tasks.
Administrators and developers may create one-time maintenance tasks.
detection_notes: T1053.005 Scheduled Task persistence is indicated when Task Content contains <Command>powershell.exe</Command>, mshta.exe, rundll32.exe, or a path under Users/Public/AppData, or when Principal RunLevel is HighestAvailable or SYSTEM. These values define executable persistence rather than a benign task name.
kql_snippet:
SecurityEvent
| where EventID == 4698
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account, TaskName, TaskContent
| order by TimeGenerated desc
spl_snippet:
index=wineventlog EventCode=4698
| table _time, host, SubjectUserName, TaskName, TaskContent
sample_log:
Task Name: \Microsoft\Windows\Update\CacheRefresh
Task Content: <Exec><Command>powershell.exe</Command><Arguments>-File C:\Users\Public\refresh.ps1</Arguments></Exec>
Subject: CORP\admin.ops
